dsl_rule.translation_notes: Contrarian reversion signal: Long entry when price drops >15% (returns < -0.15) with elevated volatility; Short entry when price spikes >15% (returns > 0.15) with elevated volatility. Exit when prior extreme is broken (close crosses back through prev_low/prev_high). Used 3-day returns to capture the >15% move over lookback_days=3. High volatility confirmed via hvol_20 > 0.15 (15% threshold). Position sizing and 1:1 profit target are implementation parameters, not condition-based.

backtest_rule.reason: Contrarian reversion signal: Long entry when price drops >15% (returns < -0.15) with elevated volatility; Short entry when price spikes >15% (returns > 0.15) with elevated volatility. Exit when prior extreme is broken (close crosses back through prev_low/prev_high). Used 3-day returns to capture the >15% move over lookback_days=3. High volatility confirmed via hvol_20 > 0.15 (15% threshold). Position sizing and 1:1 profit target are implementation parameters, not condition-based.

source_rule_text: A trader should take contrarian positions against extreme price moves (hysteria) when charts show incredible spikes, because exhaustion patterns often precede reversions.
